task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the threat_intel.csv lookup file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Splunk search interface screenshot showing an SPL query that analyzes 'Failed password' events, calculates threat levels, and ends with `| outputlookup threat_intel.csv`. The results table should display `src_ip`, `attempt_count`, and `threat_level` columns.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the lookup definition.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk Settings > Lookups > Lookup definitions page showing an entry named `threat_intel_lookup` that is associated with the `threat_intel.csv` file.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the automatic lookup.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk Settings > Lookups > Automatic lookups page showing an entry named `threat_auto_enrich` configured for the `linux_secure` sourcetype, using `threat_intel_lookup`.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the Threat_Intelligence_Monitor dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Classic Dashboard named `Threat_Intelligence_Monitor` containing a time range picker and at least three panels: an event table showing enriched fields, a timechart of event counts by threat_level, and a stats table of top 10 source IPs.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the Critical_Threat_Activity alert.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An alert configuration modal or the Alerts listing page showing an alert named `Critical_Threat_Activity` with a cron schedule of `*/15 * * * *`.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Splunk Web UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Splunk Web interface in a browser (http://localhost:8000), not just terminal windows or API scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the data shown is from the actual dataset.
integrity.visual_evidence: Search results and dashboard panels should display realistic IP addresses and attempt counts (e.g., top IP 59.63.188.30 with thousands of attempts), not empty or hardcoded mock data.

integrity.description: Verify the agent didn't bypass the UI for configurations.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should configure lookups, dashboards, and alerts through the Splunk UI, not by manually editing .conf files in a terminal text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target index: 'security_logs'. Target sourcetype: 'linux_secure'. Lookup filename: 'threat_intel.csv'. Lookup definition name: 'threat_intel_lookup'. Automatic lookup name: 'threat_auto_enrich'. Dashboard name: 'Threat_Intelligence_Monitor' (must have >= 3 panels). Alert name: 'Critical_Threat_Activity' (cron: '*/15 * * * *'). Threat thresholds: critical (>500), high (>100), medium (>20), low (otherwise). The top attacking IP in the dataset is 59.63.188.30 with 28,766 attempts.
